name: Curity
abstract: Proprietary enterprise CIAM and API security platform from Sweden. FAPI-certified, OpenID Foundation compliant, SOC 2 Type 2 and ISO 27001 certified. Unique strength in financial-grade API security, phantom tokens, and AI agent identity (Access Intelligence). Community Edition available; full feature set requires paid tier.

pricing.freeTierLimit: Community Edition: username/password, social login, complete OAuth Server

featureList.description: Built-in authenticators for Facebook, Google, Twitter/X, Sign in with Apple, and any generic OIDC or SAML 2.0 identity provider.
WebAuthn passkeys supported as a first or second factor via the dedicated WebAuthn/Passkey authenticator.
Email OTP and email back-channel authenticators enable passwordless email-based login.
SMS OTP authenticator enables passwordless phone-based login.
Email back-channel authenticator can be configured to deliver one-time login links. No dedicated magic-link authenticator out of the box.
Curity requires user identity for authentication. Anonymous or guest sessions are not a built-in feature.
TOTP is explicitly supported. HOTP (counter-based OTP) is not separately documented.
FIDO2/WebAuthn security keys support U2F-style authentication.
Email OTP authenticator usable as second factor in an MFA chain.
SMS OTP authenticator usable as second factor in an MFA chain.
Recovery codes are not explicitly documented as a built-in feature. Custom authenticator plugins can implement this.
Native push notification MFA is not built-in. Cisco Duo integration provides push notifications via the Duo authenticator.
Conditional MFA actions support risk-based triggering on any context: time of day, location, device, user attributes, or custom logic.
Built-in Duo authenticator for Cisco Duo MFA integration.
Step-up authentication via conditional MFA actions triggered by scope, ACR, or custom policy.
SAML 2.0 IdP profile available in the Enterprise tier.
OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant natively supported.
Generic OIDC authenticator for federating with any OpenID Connect identity provider.
SAML 2.0 authenticator for federating with Entra ID, ADFS, and other SAML identity providers.
LDAP/Active Directory data source for user authentication and attribute lookup.
Azure AD/Entra ID federation via OIDC or SAML authenticator. PingFederate integration also available.
Bulk user import via SCIM 2.0 API.
Password hash import is not explicitly documented in the SCIM or user management APIs.
Bulk user export via SCIM 2.0 and GraphQL APIs.
Bulk update and delete via SCIM 2.0 PATCH and DELETE operations.
SCIM 2.0 PATCH supports upsert-style operations.
Username constraints depend on the configured data source (JDBC, LDAP). No universal legacy username normalization built in.
Credential data can be imported at the data source level. No dedicated bulk MFA enrollment import API is documented.
Curity exposes a SCIM 2.0 server for inbound user provisioning from HR systems or identity platforms.
Curity can act as a SCIM client to sync users to downstream systems via the SCIM data source.
SCIM 2.0 Groups resource supported alongside Users.
Just-in-time provisioning during first federated login via authentication actions.
Lazy migration can be achieved via the Dynamic Authenticator or a custom authenticator plugin that proxies credential validation to a legacy system.
Self-Service Portal application for user profile, credential, and MFA device management.
Account linking across multiple identity providers supported via authentication profile configuration.
User accounts can be disabled via the SCIM API or GraphQL user management API.
Custom user attributes stored in the configured data source (JDBC, LDAP, SCIM, DynamoDB, MongoDB).
Custom client attributes managed via the Database Clients GraphQL API.
Attribute size limits are determined by the backend data source (database column sizes, LDAP attribute limits). No universal documented limit.
User search via SCIM 2.0 filter queries and GraphQL API.
Role-based access control via OAuth 2.0 scopes and custom claims mapped from user attributes.
Multi-tenancy via separate authentication profiles per tenant. No built-in organisation management UI; tenant isolation is infrastructure-level.
Password policies configurable via the credential manager.
Progressive profiling via authentication actions that conditionally collect additional user attributes during login.
Attribute-based access control via token procedures and custom claims enrichment.
Fine-grained authorization via OAuth 2.0 scopes, Rich Authorization Requests (for VCI), and token procedures.
Core use case: token-based API security with phantom tokens, split tokens, and sender-constrained tokens.
User data export via SCIM 2.0 and GraphQL APIs. PII data minimization and audit trails built in.
User deletion via SCIM DELETE and GraphQL APIs. Audit records support right-to-erasure workflows.
OAuth 2.0 consent delegation audited by the authorization server. Consent can be configured per-flow.
Self-hosted deployment in any region. Data residency fully controlled by the operator.
Supports on-premise, Docker, Kubernetes (AWS EKS, Azure AKS, GCP GKE), and hybrid deployments.
Audit logging via Apache Log4j 2 in Syslog RFC 5424 structured data format. Retention is operator-configured.
Structured audit logs and OpenTelemetry metrics/tracing export supported in version 10.0+.
Encryption at rest via database-level TDE or filesystem encryption. HSM support available for key material.
TLS enforced for all endpoints. Mutual TLS (mTLS) supported for client authentication.
Full key management via self-hosted deployment and HSM integration.
No built-in CAPTCHA or bot detection. Throttling framework limits request rates from misbehaving clients.
Throttling framework provides configurable brute-force protection at endpoint and credential level.
Throttling framework supports per-IP rate limiting and request rejection.
No built-in Have I Been Pwned integration documented.
IP-based access control configurable at the infrastructure level; profile-level restrictions via Curity configuration.
Geo-location and context-based MFA triggering is documented. Explicit device fingerprinting is not a built-in feature.
Full theme customization per application profile, supporting different branding per tenant or application.
MFA policies and authentication flows configured independently per authentication profile.
HAAPI (Hypermedia Authentication API) enables native embedded authentication flows without browser redirects in mobile and SPA apps.
Full UI theming and branding customization via the UI Kit and template system.
Multi-language support with localized login pages and email templates.
Curity UI Kit provides a component library for React and pre-built login and self-service portal templates.
Structured audit events and OpenTelemetry metrics available. No built-in analytics dashboard; external tools (Grafana, ELK) recommended.
Java Plugins SDK is the primary extension framework. iOS, Android, and JavaScript SDKs available for HAAPI and Token Handler patterns.
RESTCONF API for configuration management, GraphQL APIs for client and user management.
Throttling configurable per endpoint, client, and credential to enforce authentication request rate limits.
Extensible via Java Plugins SDK: custom authenticators, authentication actions, token procedures, claims providers, event listeners, and data source plugins. Groovy scripting also supported.
Extensibility is Java-based (SDK plugins) or Groovy scripts. No TypeScript/JavaScript runtime for server-side logic.
Self-hosted deployment supports any custom domain.
CLI scripting via RESTCONF API and commit hooks for GitOps workflows and CI/CD automation.
No official Terraform provider available. Infrastructure management relies on RESTCONF API, CLI scripts, and Helm charts for Kubernetes deployments.
Multiple data source types: JDBC (PostgreSQL, MySQL, etc.), LDAP, SCIM, JSON/REST, DynamoDB, MongoDB, and CouchDB.
Event listener plugins and server events system support webhook notifications. Webhook alarm handler available for operational alerts.
Full HTML/CSS customization via the UI builder, template system, and Curity UI Kit.
Custom SMTP server and pluggable email provider via the Java Plugins SDK.
Fully customizable email templates for OTP, verification, and notification flows.
Custom claims via token procedures (Groovy scripts) and claims value provider plugins.
Authentication flows are configured via the admin UI and XML configuration. No visual drag-and-drop flow builder.
Built-in support for strong national eIDs: Swedish BankID, SITHS (healthcare), and Entrust IDaaS. No general document verification.
OpenID Wallet authenticator and verifiable credential issuance support experimental decentralized identity use cases.
Access Intelligence and Token Intelligence features provide scoped, auditable tokens for AI agents, automated workflows, and machine-to-machine API access with ephemeral client support.
